# VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0018 - QUIC RFC 9000 Application ACK Piggyback Policy Verification

## Scope

This verification covers active-path 1-RTT Application Data packet composition when ACK-generation state already contains pending ack-eliciting Application Data receipts, plus ACK-only feedback-loop protection for the same supported lane.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0010
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0012

## Verification Method

Requirement-home execution, deterministic fuzz/property sampling, BenchmarkDotNet evidence, SpecTrace render/check, attempted JSON validation, and diff hygiene for the bounded active-path 1-RTT Application Data ACK piggyback policy.

## Preconditions

- 1-RTT Application Data packet protection material is available.
- The active path is validated and can send ordinary packets.
- ACK-generation state has already recorded pending Application Data packet receipts before the piggyback composition check.
- Initial, Handshake, candidate-path, endpoint interop, public API, and general delayed-ACK timer behavior are outside this verification.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the focused RFC 9000 requirement-home tests for S13P2P1-0009, S13P2P1-0010, and S13P2P1-0012.
- Run the ACK piggyback policy benchmark suite with BenchmarkDotNet Dry and Short jobs and preserve logs and reports.
- Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
- Run canonical SpecTrace JSON validation and preserve the output, including standing repository-wide residual failures if present.
- Run `git diff --check`.

## Expected Result

Focused tests prove that pending Application Data ACK state is piggybacked onto outbound stream-open and stream-write packets, no ACK frame is invented when no ACK is pending, ACK-only packets emitted after inbound 1-RTT PING contain only ACK plus padding and remain non-ack-eliciting/non-retransmittable, and a sent piggybacked ACK suppresses duplicate ACK-only or repeated piggyback triggers until a newer ack-eliciting packet arrives. BenchmarkDotNet Dry and Short jobs validate the permanent ACK piggyback policy benchmark suite without making public or MSQUIC comparison claims. SpecTrace render/check must pass for the touched artifacts; repository-wide JSON validation output is preserved even if the current core profile still reports standing residual Markdown and unresolved-reference backlog outside the focused runtime proof.

## Status

Focused runtime, benchmark, render/check, and diff-hygiene proof passed locally on 2026-04-25 for the bounded active-path 1-RTT Application Data ACK piggyback policy floor. Repository-wide SpecTrace JSON validation still reports standing residual Markdown and unresolved-reference backlog; the failure log is preserved as evidence rather than reclassified as a focused behavior failure.

## Benchmark Boundary

The benchmark evidence is an internal Incursa-only policy benchmark for ACK-generation checks used by the active-path 1-RTT piggyback composition floor. It is not a public facade, System.Net.Quic, MSQUIC, HTTP/3, or interop comparison.
